celebration: 🎉 THE BIG ONE — 'ON' IS DECODABLE. You have said this word all year — now you can READ it: o-n, on. AND the vowel wall lights up its third vowel (A · I · O). Two celebrations in one landmark week.

songs.title: O-O-On
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
O! O! …oh? No!
Ah! Ah! Ah-ah-on!
(ah… ah… ah…)

[Hook]
Ah! Ah! Ah-ah-ah!
What begins with ah?
Ah-ah-octopus! Ah-ah-octopus!
It's an octopus! (an octopus!)

[Verse 2]
Ah! Ah! Ah-ah-ah!
What begins with ah?
Ah-ah-olive! Ah-ah-olive!
It's an olive! (an olive!)

[Verse 3]
Ah! Ah! Ah-ah-ah!
What begins with ah?
Ah-ah-ox! Ah-ah-ox!
It's an ox! (an ox!)

[Verse 4]
Ah! Ah! Ah-ah-ah!
What begins with ah?
Ah-ah-otter! Ah-ah-otter!
It's an otter! (an otter!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not ah!
(but O-N spells ON — you can read it!)
(ah… ah… ah…)

[Outro]
Ah-ah-on! Ah-ah-on!
ON! You can read it!
Ah! Ah! Ah-ah-on!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: SHORT vowel /ŏ/ as in 'on'/'hot' — the lyrics spell it 'ah' so Suno never sings the letter name 'oh'. The intro's 'oh? No! Ah!' is the joke: correcting the letter name to the true sound. The printed classroom letter stays 'o' — 'ah' is Suno-only respelling.

songs.title: The Pot Is Hot
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
(hot… hot… the pot is hot…)

[Hook]
The pot is hot! The pot is hot!
Is it hot? (it's HOT!)
The pot is hot! The pot is hot!
Not, not, NOT on the pot!

[Verse 2]
The cat is on the pot! (get off!)
The cat is on the pot!
Hot, hot! Not on the pot!
Hop off the pot, cat!

[Bridge — THE CELEBRATION]
ON! You can read it!
O-N — on!
On the mat! On the pot!
You said it all year…
now you READ it!
(ON! ON! ON!)

[Verse 3]
The pot is on top! (up high!)
The pot is on top!
It is not hot. (phew…)
The pot is on top.

[Outro]
The pot is not hot.
(all is calm…)
A potato… in the pot?!
(POTATO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: 🎉 THE LANDMARK ANTHEM. Frame: 'The pot is hot!' with the hot/not/on/top wordplay. The BRIDGE is the whole point — the class realises they can finally READ 'on', a word they've spoken since Week 1. Sing the bridge big and bright, the rest playful-spooky. The potato bobs up in the pot at the very end for the loud finish.

spreads.artNote: A round black cooking pot alone on a stovetop, spotlit — the star object. Calm before the heat.
The pot now steaming, a warm red-orange glow from below, little heat lines rising. Danger, but cosy-danger.
Sam the peg-doll boy reaching curiously toward the pot; Dad's big hand gently pulling him back. A real safety beat, warm not scary.

spreads.text: The cat sat on the pot!
spreads.artNote: The tabby cat has hopped up and is sitting right on the pot lid, oblivious, tail curled. Everyone's eyes go wide.

spreads.text: Hot! Hot! Not on the pot!
spreads.artNote: The cat launches straight up off the lid in a comic startled leap, all four legs out, ears back. The 'hot/not' punchline.
Dad sets the pot safely up high on a shelf, out of reach. Order restored. 'top' anchored.

spreads.text: It is not hot. It is a pot.
spreads.artNote: The pot cool again on the table, the cat sniffing it warily from a safe distance, no more steam. Calm.

spreads.text: on! on! on! The cat is on the mat.
spreads.artNote: 🎉 THE CELEBRATION SPREAD. A giant gold 'ON' fills the page; the whole cast — Dad, Sam, Segina, the cat, the ant — point at it, cheering, reading the word they've said all year. Fireworks of light. This is the year's second great moment after the first decodable book.
The potato bobbing happily in the (now-cool) pot like a hot tub, deadpan. The cat peers over the rim. Class shouts the word.

sentences: The cat is not on the pot.
It is hot! It is on top.
extras: 🎉 Vowel wall: the O poster LIGHTS UP — gold star added beside A and I (three of five vowels now glowing)
🎉 'ON' celebration card: o-n built in the moveable alphabet, then read in real sentences ('on the mat', 'on top') — the word said all year, now READ
Bingo Vowel Song ritual at the wall: 'A-E-I-O-U' with A, I and O now glowing
Moveable alphabet: build o-n, n-o-t, t-o-p, p-o-t, h-o-t, h-o-p
Sandpaper letter 'o' + sand tray — short round /ŏ/
'Hot pot / not on the pot' cat-hop game — read where the cat is, hop it off the hot pot onto the mat

teacherFocus: 🎉 The landmark week — two celebrations. First: 'on' becomes decodable. The class has SAID this word since Week 1; now they can READ it (o-n, on). Build it, read it in real sentences ('on the mat', 'on top'), and make the moment as big as the first decodable book — this is the year's second summit. Second: the O lights up on the vowel wall, the third of five vowels (A · I · O now glowing). Teach the short round /ŏ/ as in 'on' and 'pot', never 'oh'. Blend o-n, n-o-t, t-o-p, p-o-t. The book — a pot that gets hot, a cat that sits on it and leaps off — carries a real gentle safety lesson and lands on the giant gold 'ON' celebration spread. Sing 'The Pot Is Hot' loud, and let the bridge — 'you said it all year, now you read it' — be the emotional peak.
